leadText: Upper Hand (Japanese: はやてがえし Quick Hand Reversal) is a damage-dealing Fighting-type move introduced during Generation IX, in The Indigo Disk. It is TM229 in Pokémon Scarlet and Violet.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] Upper Hand has a priority of +3. If the target has selected a physical or special move with increased priority from +1 to +3 (including Upper Hand itself) and has not yet had a chance to execute its move, Upper Hand will strike first and cause the target to flinch ; otherwise, Upper Hand will fail. It also works on Abilities that give priority to moves, such as Gale Wings .

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description S V The user reacts to the target's movement and strikes with the heel of its palm, making the target flinch. This move fails if the target is not readying a priority move.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Samurott , Cobalion , Terrakion , Virizion , Keldeo , Talonflame , Falinks , and Flamigo can learn Upper Hand despite lacking hands.
